title: Leakage-safe prefix stats plus regime-aware rank-space ensemble
summary: Your best models still use mostly frequency encodings for high-cardinality categories.
rationale: Your best models still use mostly frequency encodings for high-cardinality categories. CatBoost-style ordered target statistics are a more expressive way to capture driver/race/compound/stint tendencies without future leakage, and they pair well with an ensemble of orthogonal experts. For AUC, rank-space blending is usually safer than chasing raw probability calibration alone.
implementation_hint: Create strictly prefix-based or fold-wise ordered target stats for combos like `(Driver, Compound)`, `(Race, Compound, Stint)`, `(Race_Year, Driver)`, and post-pit state. Train a lightweight 'history expert' on these plus core numerics, then rank-average or logit-stack it with the base tree, survival sidecar, and sequence sidecar. If score skew hurts stacking, try beta calibration on OOF logits before blending.
expected_effect: Can add stable categorical memory and make the final ensemble less dependent on one heuristic-heavy tree model.
risk: The gain disappears if ordered stats are not truly OOF/causal. Beta calibration can also overfit if applied per tiny regime; keep regimes coarse and weights simple.
